untranslatable_reason: Signal requires external inputs not available in price/technical indicators: (1) 'Fundamental value estimate from insightful analysis of non-widely-known information' — requires domain-specific valuation model external to the engine; (2) 'Information source confidence score ≥ 0.55' — requires external confidence scoring system not present in allowed indicators; (3) Exact 5% discount/premium thresholds cannot be operationalized without a fundamental value variable. The rule is inherently discretionary and requires subjective analyst judgment and external data sources (fundamental analysis, information quality scoring) that are not available as standardized technical indicators.
translation_notes: This signal describes a fundamental value reversion strategy that cannot be mechanically translated into technical indicator rules. The core thesis (buy undervalued, sell overvalued) requires either: (a) a fundamental value input parameter that varies per instrument/time, or (b) integration with an external valuation model. The 'information confidence score' and reliance on 'non-widely-known information' suggest this requires qualitative human judgment or alternative data sources outside the scope of OHLCV technical analysis. Recommend implementing as a discretionary overlay or integrating a dedicated fundamental valuation API/model into the backtest engine.
